-- lua_region_validate.lua -- LuaRegion topology invariants.

local Schema = require("lua_compile.schema")
local pvm = require("lalin.pvm")
local T = Schema.get()
local Region = T.LuaRegion

local M = {}

local function add(errors, msg) errors[#errors + 1] = msg end

function M.validate(region_set)
  local errors = {}
  if pvm.classof(region_set) ~= Region.RegionSet then add(errors, "expected LuaRegion.RegionSet") end
  for i, r in ipairs((region_set and region_set.regions) or {}) do
    local cls = pvm.classof(r)
    if not Region.Region.members[cls] then add(errors, "region " .. i .. " is not LuaRegion.Region") end
    if #(r.edges or {}) == 0 then add(errors, "region " .. i .. " has no edges") end
    if r.slots and r.slots.first.id > r.slots.last.id then add(errors, "region " .. i .. " has invalid slot window") end
  end
  return #errors == 0, errors
end

return M

-- lua_fact_validate.lua -- evidence-layer checks.

local Validate = require("lua_compile.validate")

local M = {}
function M.validate(evidence) return Validate.lua_fact_evidence(evidence) end
return M

-- lua_fact_contradiction.lua -- contradiction detection for LuaFact evidence.

local Schema = require("lua_compile.schema")
local T = Schema.get()
local Fact = T.LuaFact

local M = {}

local TYPE_PRED = { [Fact.IsNil]=true, [Fact.IsFalse]=true, [Fact.IsTrue]=true, [Fact.IsBool]=true, [Fact.IsI64]=true, [Fact.IsF64]=true, [Fact.IsTable]=true, [Fact.IsClosure]=true }

local function subject_key(s)
  if not s then return "?" end
  if s.kind == "SrcSlot" then return "slot:" .. s.slot.id end
  if s.kind == "CanonSlot" then return "canon:" .. s.slot_class end
  if s.kind == "Const" then return "const:" .. s.k.id end
  if s.kind == "Global" then return "global" end
  return tostring(s.kind)
end

function M.find(evidence)
  local by_subject = {}
  local errors = {}
  for _, f in ipairs((evidence and evidence.observed) or {}) do
    if TYPE_PRED[f.predicate] then
      local k = subject_key(f.subject)
      by_subject[k] = by_subject[k] or {}
      by_subject[k][f.predicate.kind] = true
    end
  end
  for subject, preds in pairs(by_subject) do
    local concrete = {}
    for pred in pairs(preds) do if pred ~= "IsNumber" and pred ~= "IsBool" then concrete[#concrete + 1] = pred end end
    if #concrete > 1 then table.sort(concrete); errors[#errors + 1] = "contradictory type facts for " .. subject .. ": " .. table.concat(concrete, ",") end
  end
  return errors
end

return M

-- lua_fact_closure.lua -- implication/dependency closure for LuaFact evidence.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local T = B.T
local Fact = T.LuaFact

local M = {}

local function key(f) return tostring(f.subject and f.subject.kind) .. ":" .. tostring(f.subject and (f.subject.slot and f.subject.slot.id or f.subject.slot_class or f.subject.id or "")) .. ":" .. tostring(f.predicate and f.predicate.kind) .. ":" .. tostring(f.value_key) end
local function add(out, seen, f) local k = key(f); if not seen[k] then seen[k] = true; out[#out + 1] = f end end

function M.close(evidence)
  local out, seen = {}, {}
  for _, f in ipairs((evidence and evidence.observed) or {}) do
    add(out, seen, f)
    if f.predicate == Fact.ShapeEq or f.predicate == Fact.ShapeKnown then add(out, seen, Fact.Fact(f.subject, Fact.IsTable, "", f.deps or {})) end
    if f.predicate == Fact.FieldOffset then add(out, seen, Fact.Fact(f.subject, Fact.KeyConst, f.value_key or "", f.deps or {})) end
  end
  return Fact.Evidence(out, evidence and evidence.payloads or {}, evidence and evidence.regions or B.region_set({}))
end

return M

-- lua_fact_from_runtime_observe.lua -- runtime observations -> LuaFact.Evidence.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local Fact = B.LuaFact
local Closure = require("lua_compile.lua_fact_closure")

local M = {}

local PRED = {
  is_i64 = Fact.IsI64, i64 = Fact.IsI64,
  is_f64 = Fact.IsF64, f64 = Fact.IsF64,
  is_table = Fact.IsTable, table = Fact.IsTable,
  nonzero_i64 = Fact.NonZeroI64,
  const_i64 = Fact.ConstI64,
  key_const = Fact.KeyConst,
  barrier_clean = Fact.BarrierClean,
}

local function subject(obs)
  if obs.subject then return obs.subject end
  if obs.slot ~= nil then return Fact.SrcSlot(B.slot(obs.slot)) end
  if obs.k ~= nil or obs.const ~= nil then return Fact.Const(B.k(obs.k or obs.const)) end
  return Fact.Global
end

function M.observe(observations, regions)
  local facts = {}
  for _, obs in ipairs(observations or {}) do
    local pred = PRED[obs.predicate or obs.kind] or Fact[obs.predicate or obs.kind]
    if pred then facts[#facts + 1] = Fact.Fact(subject(obs), pred, tostring(obs.value_key or obs.value or ""), obs.deps or {}) end
  end
  return Closure.close(Fact.Evidence(facts, {}, regions or B.region_set({})))
end

return M

-- lua_fact_from_foundry_bundle.lua -- foundry bundles -> LuaFact.Evidence.
--
-- This consumes fact records as evidence input only. It is not an adapter to the
-- old compiler, bank, descriptor, or materializer APIs.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local Fact = B.LuaFact
local Closure = require("lua_compile.lua_fact_closure")

local M = {}

local PRED = {
  is_nil=Fact.IsNil, is_false=Fact.IsFalse, is_true=Fact.IsTrue, is_bool=Fact.IsBool,
  is_i64=Fact.IsI64, is_f64=Fact.IsF64, is_number=Fact.IsNumber, is_table=Fact.IsTable,
  is_closure=Fact.IsClosure, nonzero_i64=Fact.NonZeroI64, const_i64=Fact.ConstI64,
  const_f64=Fact.ConstF64, key_const=Fact.KeyConst, shape_known=Fact.ShapeKnown,
  shape_eq=Fact.ShapeEq, metatable_absent=Fact.MetatableAbsent, field_offset=Fact.FieldOffset,
  array_hit=Fact.ArrayHit, bounds_ok=Fact.BoundsOk, array_base_offset=Fact.ArrayBaseOffset,
  array_len_offset=Fact.ArrayLenOffset, known_call_target=Fact.KnownCallTarget,
  target_eq=Fact.TargetEq, barrier_clean=Fact.BarrierClean,
}

local DEP = { shape_epoch=Fact.ShapeEpoch, metatable_epoch=Fact.MetatableEpoch, const_epoch=Fact.ConstEpoch, upvalue_epoch=Fact.UpvalueEpoch, table_epoch=Fact.TableEpoch, call_target_epoch=Fact.CallTargetEpoch, gc_barrier_protocol=Fact.GcBarrierProtocol, vm_abi_epoch=Fact.VmAbiEpoch }

local function deps(xs)
  local out = {}
  for _, d in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[#out + 1] = DEP[d] or d end
  return out
end

local function subject(s)
  if type(s) == "table" and s.kind == "slot" then
    local n = tostring(s.id or "0"):match("R?(%d+)") or 0
    return Fact.SrcSlot(B.slot(n))
  elseif type(s) == "table" and s.kind == "value" then
    return Fact.CanonSlot(tonumber(tostring(s.id):match("(%d+)")) or 0)
  elseif type(s) == "table" and s.kind == "global" then
    return Fact.Global
  elseif type(s) == "string" and s:match("^R?%d+$") then
    return Fact.SrcSlot(B.slot(s:match("(%d+)")))
  end
  return Fact.Global
end

function M.from_bundle(bundle, regions)
  local facts, payloads = {}, {}
  for _, f in ipairs(bundle or {}) do
    local pred = PRED[f.predicate]
    if pred then facts[#facts + 1] = Fact.Fact(subject(f.subject), pred, tostring(f.value_key or f.value or ""), deps(f.deps)) end
  end
  return Closure.close(Fact.Evidence(facts, payloads, regions or B.region_set({})))
end

return M

-- lua_sem_boundary.lua -- exact boundary observations.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local Src, Sem = B.LuaSrc, B.LuaSem

local M = {}

local REASON_BY_OP = {
  LOADKX = Src.ExtraArgBoundary,
  LFALSESKIP = Src.BranchSkipBoundary,
  EQ = Src.ComparisonBoundary, LT = Src.ComparisonBoundary, LE = Src.ComparisonBoundary,
  EQK = Src.ComparisonBoundary, EQI = Src.ComparisonBoundary, LTI = Src.ComparisonBoundary, LEI = Src.ComparisonBoundary, GTI = Src.ComparisonBoundary, GEI = Src.ComparisonBoundary,
  TEST = Src.TestBranchBoundary, TESTSET = Src.TestBranchBoundary,
  MMBIN = Src.MetamethodBoundary, MMBINI = Src.MetamethodBoundary, MMBINK = Src.MetamethodBoundary,
  LEN = Src.LengthBoundary, CONCAT = Src.ConcatBoundary,
  CLOSE = Src.CloseBoundary, TBC = Src.TbcBoundary,
  NEWTABLE = Src.AllocationBoundary, SETLIST = Src.BulkTableBoundary, CLOSURE = Src.ClosureBoundary,
  VARARG = Src.VarargBoundary, GETVARG = Src.VarargBoundary, VARARGPREP = Src.VarargBoundary,
  ERRNNIL = Src.ErrorBoundary,
  CALL = Src.CallBoundary, TAILCALL = Src.CallBoundary,
  RETURN = Src.ReturnBoundary, RETURN0 = Src.ReturnBoundary,
  JMP = Src.JumpBoundary,
  FORPREP = Src.NumericForRegionBoundary, FORLOOP = Src.NumericForRegionBoundary,
  TFORPREP = Src.GenericForRegionBoundary, TFORCALL = Src.GenericForRegionBoundary, TFORLOOP = Src.GenericForRegionBoundary,
  EXTRAARG = Src.ExtraArgBoundary,
}

function M.reason_for_op(op)
  return REASON_BY_OP[op and op.kind] or Src.VmBoundary
end

function M.observe(op, reason)
  return Sem.Observe(Sem.BoundaryObservation(op.pc, reason or M.reason_for_op(op)))
end

return M

-- lua_sem_validate.lua -- semantic-layer invariants.

local Validate = require("lua_compile.validate")
local Schema = require("lua_compile.schema")
local pvm = require("lalin.pvm")
local T = Schema.get()

local M = {}

function M.validate(result)
  local ok, errors = Validate.lua_sem_result(result)
  if ok and pvm.classof(result) == T.LuaSem.Accepted then
    for i, eff in ipairs(result.program.effects or {}) do
      if not T.LuaSem.Effect.members[pvm.classof(eff)] then errors[#errors + 1] = "effect " .. i .. " is not LuaSem.Effect" end
    end
  end
  return #errors == 0, errors
end

return M

-- lua_src_to_lua_sem_lower.lua -- LuaSrc.Window + LuaFact.Evidence -> LuaSem.Result.
--
-- LuaSem consumes opcode meaning. Unsupported cases are structured rejections,
-- not fallback helpers or descriptor stubs.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local T = B.T
local Src, Sem, Fact = T.LuaSrc, T.LuaSem, T.LuaFact
local Env = require("lua_compile.lua_sem_env")
local Guard = require("lua_compile.lua_sem_guard")
local Write = require("lua_compile.lua_sem_write")
local Boundary = require("lua_compile.lua_sem_boundary")
local Reject = require("lua_compile.lua_sem_reject")
local Contradiction = require("lua_compile.lua_fact_contradiction")

local M = {}

local function subject_key(s)
  if s.kind == "SrcSlot" then return "slot:" .. s.slot.id end
  if s.kind == "Const" then return "const:" .. s.k.id end
  if s.kind == "CanonSlot" then return "canon:" .. s.slot_class end
  if s.kind == "Global" then return "global" end
  return tostring(s.kind)
end

local function evidence_index(evidence)
  local idx = {}
  for _, f in ipairs((evidence and evidence.observed) or {}) do
    idx[subject_key(f.subject) .. ":" .. f.predicate.kind] = f
  end
  return idx
end
local function has(idx, subject, pred) return idx[subject_key(subject) .. ":" .. pred.kind] end
local function missing(subject, pred) return Fact.Fact(subject, pred, "", {}) end

local function i64_atom_for_slot(env, slot)
  env:alias(slot)
  return Sem.AtomI64(Sem.SlotI64(env:slot_class(slot)))
end
local function i64_imm(n) return Sem.AtomI64(Sem.ImmI64(type(n) == "table" and n or B.imm(n))) end
local function i64_const(k) return Sem.AtomI64(Sem.ConstI64(type(k) == "table" and k or B.k(k))) end
local function box_i64(x) return Sem.BoxI64(x) end
local function affine(terms, constant) return Sem.AffineI64(terms or {}, constant or 0) end
local function term(c, atom)
  if atom.kind == "AtomI64" then atom = atom.atom end
  return Sem.I64Term(c, atom)
end

local function const_i64_from_evidence(idx, k)
  local f = has(idx, Fact.Const(k), Fact.ConstI64)
  if f and tostring(f.value_key or ""):match("^-?%d+$") then return tonumber(f.value_key) end
  return nil
end

local BOUNDARY_OP = {
  LOADKX=true, LFALSESKIP=true, JMP=true, CALL=true, TAILCALL=true, RETURN=true, RETURN0=true,
  FORPREP=true, FORLOOP=true, TFORPREP=true, TFORCALL=true, TFORLOOP=true,
  EQ=true, LT=true, LE=true, EQK=true, EQI=true, LTI=true, LEI=true, GTI=true, GEI=true, TEST=true, TESTSET=true,
  MMBIN=true, MMBINI=true, MMBINK=true, LEN=true, CONCAT=true, CLOSE=true, TBC=true, NEWTABLE=true, SETLIST=true, CLOSURE=true,
  VARARG=true, GETVARG=true, ERRNNIL=true, VARARGPREP=true, EXTRAARG=true,
}

local function require_i64(idx, op, slot, effects)
  local subject = Fact.SrcSlot(slot)
  local f = has(idx, subject, Fact.IsI64)
  if not f then return nil, missing(subject, Fact.IsI64) end
  effects[#effects + 1] = Guard.observe(Guard.fact_guard(subject, Fact.IsI64, Sem.SlotValue(Sem.SlotClass(slot.id)), f.deps or {}, op.pc))
  return true
end

function M.lower(window, evidence)
  local contradictions = Contradiction.find(evidence)
  if #contradictions > 0 then
    local op = window and window.ops and window.ops[1]
    return Reject.reject(op or Src.UnsupportedOpcode(B.pc(0), "<empty>"), "contradictory_evidence")
  end

  local idx = evidence_index(evidence)
  local env = Env.new()
  local effects = {}

  for _, op in ipairs((window and window.ops) or {}) do
    if op.a then env:alias(op.a, op.pc, op.pc) end
    if op.lhs then env:alias(op.lhs, op.pc, op.pc) end
    if op.rhs and op.rhs.id then env:alias(op.rhs, op.pc, op.pc) end
    if op.b then env:alias(op.b, op.pc, op.pc) end

    if op.kind == "LOADI" then
      effects[#effects + 1] = Write.slot(env:slot_class(op.a), box_i64(i64_imm(op.value)))
    elseif op.kind == "MOVE" then
      effects[#effects + 1] = Write.slot(env:slot_class(op.a), env:slot_value(op.b))
    elseif op.kind == "ADDI" then
      local ok, miss = require_i64(idx, op, op.lhs, effects); if not ok then return Reject.reject(op, "missing_fact", { miss }) end
      local rhs = op.rhs.value
      effects[#effects + 1] = Write.slot(env:slot_class(op.a), box_i64(affine({ term(1, i64_atom_for_slot(env, op.lhs)) }, rhs)))
    elseif op.kind == "ADDK" or op.kind == "SUBK" then
      local ok, miss = require_i64(idx, op, op.lhs, effects); if not ok then return Reject.reject(op, "missing_fact", { miss }) end
      local kval = const_i64_from_evidence(idx, op.rhs)
      if not kval then return Reject.reject(op, "missing_fact", { missing(Fact.Const(op.rhs), Fact.ConstI64) }) end
      if op.kind == "SUBK" then kval = -kval end
      effects[#effects + 1] = Write.slot(env:slot_class(op.a), box_i64(affine({ term(1, i64_atom_for_slot(env, op.lhs)) }, kval)))
    elseif op.kind == "ADD" or op.kind == "SUB" then
      local ok1, miss1 = require_i64(idx, op, op.lhs, effects); if not ok1 then return Reject.reject(op, "missing_fact", { miss1 }) end
      local ok2, miss2 = require_i64(idx, op, op.rhs, effects); if not ok2 then return Reject.reject(op, "missing_fact", { miss2 }) end
      local coeff = op.kind == "SUB" and -1 or 1
      effects[#effects + 1] = Write.slot(env:slot_class(op.a), box_i64(affine({ term(1, i64_atom_for_slot(env, op.lhs)), term(coeff, i64_atom_for_slot(env, op.rhs)) }, 0)))
    elseif op.kind == "RETURN1" then
      env:alias(op.value, op.pc, op.pc)
      effects[#effects + 1] = Sem.Observe(Sem.ReturnObservation(op.pc, env:slot_value(op.value)))
    elseif BOUNDARY_OP[op.kind] then
      effects[#effects + 1] = Boundary.observe(op)
    elseif op.kind == "UnsupportedOpcode" then
      return Reject.reject(op, "unsupported_opcode")
    else
      return Reject.reject(op, "unsupported_opcode")
    end
  end

  return Sem.Accepted(Sem.Program(env:aliases_array(), effects))
end

return M

-- lua_nf_expr_canonicalize.lua -- arithmetic/value canonical forms.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local T = B.T
local Sem, NF = T.LuaSem, T.LuaNF

local M = {}

local function atom_key(a)
  if a.kind == "SrcSlotI64" then return "S" .. a.slot.id end
  if a.kind == "ImmI64" then return "I" .. a.imm.value end
  if a.kind == "ConstI64" then return "K" .. a.k.id end
  return tostring(a.kind)
end

function M.affine(terms, constant)
  local by_key, atoms = {}, {}
  for _, t in ipairs(terms or {}) do
    local k = atom_key(t.atom)
    by_key[k] = (by_key[k] or 0) + t.coefficient
    atoms[k] = t.atom
  end
  local keys, out = {}, {}
  for k, c in pairs(by_key) do if c ~= 0 then keys[#keys + 1] = k end end
  table.sort(keys)
  for _, k in ipairs(keys) do out[#out + 1] = NF.I64Term(by_key[k], atoms[k]) end
  return NF.CanonAffineI64(out, constant or 0)
end

return M

-- lua_nf_guard_reduce.lua -- redundant guard reduction.

local M = {}
function M.reduce(guards)
  local seen, out = {}, {}
  for _, g in ipairs(guards or {}) do
    local k = tostring(g.kind) .. ":" .. tostring(g.subject and g.subject.kind) .. ":" .. tostring(g.predicate and g.predicate.kind)
    if not seen[k] then seen[k] = true; out[#out + 1] = g end
  end
  return out
end
return M

-- lua_nf_projection_reduce.lua -- minimal projection obligations.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local NF = B.LuaNF

local M = {}

function M.for_exit(slot_values)
  local out, keys = {}, {}
  for id in pairs(slot_values or {}) do keys[#keys + 1] = id end
  table.sort(keys)
  for _, id in ipairs(keys) do
    local v = slot_values[id]
    if v and v.kind == "BoxI64TValue" then out[#out + 1] = NF.LiveI64(B.LuaSem.SlotClass(id), v.value)
    elseif v then out[#out + 1] = NF.LiveTValue(B.LuaSem.SlotClass(id), v) end
  end
  return out
end

return M

-- lua_nf_validate.lua -- normal-form invariants.

local Validate = require("lua_compile.validate")
local M = {}
function M.validate(nf) return Validate.lua_nf_program(nf) end
return M

-- lua_sem_to_lua_nf_normalize.lua -- LuaSem.Program -> LuaNF.Program.

local Schema = require("lua_compile.schema")
local pvm = require("lalin.pvm")
local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local T = B.T
local Sem, NF = T.LuaSem, T.LuaNF
local Canon = require("lua_compile.lua_nf_expr_canonicalize")
local Proj = require("lua_compile.lua_nf_projection_reduce")
local WriteReduce = require("lua_compile.lua_nf_write_reduce")

local M = {}

local function atom_to_nf(a, env)
  if a.kind == "SlotI64" then
    local cur = env[a.slot.id]
    if cur and cur.kind == "BoxI64TValue" then return cur.value end
    return NF.CanonAffineI64({ NF.I64Term(1, NF.SrcSlotI64(a.slot)) }, 0)
  elseif a.kind == "ImmI64" then
    return NF.CanonAffineI64({}, a.imm.value)
  elseif a.kind == "ConstI64" then
    return NF.CanonAffineI64({ NF.I64Term(1, NF.ConstI64(a.k)) }, 0)
  elseif a.kind == "UnboxI64" then
    return NF.CanonAffineI64({ NF.I64Term(1, NF.UnboxI64(M.tvalue(a.value, env))) }, 0)
  elseif a.kind == "ValueI64" then
    return NF.CanonAffineI64({ NF.I64Term(1, NF.RefI64(NF.ValueId(a.id.id))) }, 0)
  end
  error("unsupported LuaSem.I64Atom " .. tostring(a.kind))
end

local function terms_from_i64(x, env, coeff, out)
  coeff = coeff or 1; out = out or {}
  if x.kind == "AtomI64" then
    local nx = atom_to_nf(x.atom, env)
    if nx.kind == "CanonAffineI64" then
      for _, t in ipairs(nx.terms) do out[#out + 1] = NF.I64Term(coeff * t.coefficient, t.atom) end
      return out, coeff * nx.constant
    end
  elseif x.kind == "AffineI64" then
    local const = coeff * (x.constant or 0)
    for _, t in ipairs(x.terms or {}) do
      local nx = atom_to_nf(t.atom, env)
      if nx.kind == "CanonAffineI64" then
        for _, nt in ipairs(nx.terms) do out[#out + 1] = NF.I64Term(coeff * t.coefficient * nt.coefficient, nt.atom) end
        const = const + coeff * t.coefficient * nx.constant
      end
    end
    return out, const
  end
  return { NF.I64Term(coeff, NF.RefI64(NF.ValueId(0))) }, 0
end

function M.i64(x, env)
  if x.kind == "AtomI64" or x.kind == "AffineI64" then
    local terms, const = terms_from_i64(x, env, 1, {})
    return Canon.affine(terms, const)
  elseif x.kind == "MulI64" then return NF.CanonMulI64(M.i64(x.lhs, env), M.i64(x.rhs, env))
  elseif x.kind == "IDivI64" then return NF.CanonIDivI64(M.i64(x.lhs, env), M.i64(x.rhs, env))
  elseif x.kind == "ModI64" then return NF.CanonModI64(M.i64(x.lhs, env), M.i64(x.rhs, env))
  elseif x.kind == "BitAndI64" then return NF.CanonBitAndI64({ M.i64(x.lhs, env), M.i64(x.rhs, env) })
  elseif x.kind == "BitOrI64" then return NF.CanonBitOrI64({ M.i64(x.lhs, env), M.i64(x.rhs, env) })
  elseif x.kind == "BitXorI64" then return NF.CanonBitXorI64({ M.i64(x.lhs, env), M.i64(x.rhs, env) })
  elseif x.kind == "ShlI64" then return NF.CanonShiftI64(B.LuaSrc.Shl, M.i64(x.lhs, env), M.i64(x.rhs, env))
  elseif x.kind == "ShrI64" then return NF.CanonShiftI64(B.LuaSrc.Shr, M.i64(x.lhs, env), M.i64(x.rhs, env))
  elseif x.kind == "NegI64" then return NF.CanonNegI64(M.i64(x.value, env))
  elseif x.kind == "BitNotI64" then return NF.CanonBitNotI64(M.i64(x.value, env)) end
  error("unsupported LuaSem.I64 " .. tostring(x.kind))
end

function M.tvalue(v, env)
  if v.kind == "SlotValue" then return env[v.slot.id] or NF.SrcSlotTValue(v.slot)
  elseif v.kind == "ConstValue" then return NF.ConstTValue(v.k)
  elseif v.kind == "Nil" then return NF.NilTValue
  elseif v.kind == "Bool" then return NF.BoolTValue(v.value)
  elseif v.kind == "BoxI64" then return NF.BoxI64TValue(M.i64(v.value, env))
  elseif v.kind == "BoxF64" then return NF.BoxF64TValue(NF.ImmF64(B.imm(0)))
  elseif v.kind == "UnknownTValue" then return NF.RefTValue(NF.ValueId(0)) end
  return NF.RefTValue(NF.ValueId(0))
end

local function next_exit_id(state)
  local id = state.next_exit_id; state.next_exit_id = id + 1; return NF.ExitId(id)
end

local function lower_observation(obs, state)
  if obs.kind == "GuardObservation" then
    local projection = Proj.for_exit(state.env)
    local exit = NF.GuardExit(next_exit_id(state), obs.guard.pc, projection)
    state.exits[#state.exits + 1] = exit
    state.steps[#state.steps + 1] = NF.Guard(NF.FactGuard(obs.guard.subject, obs.guard.predicate, M.tvalue(obs.guard.value, state.env), obs.guard.deps or {}, exit))
  elseif obs.kind == "BoundaryObservation" then
    local projection = Proj.for_exit(state.env)
    local exit = NF.BoundaryExit(next_exit_id(state), obs.pc, obs.reason, projection)
    state.exits[#state.exits + 1] = exit
    state.steps[#state.steps + 1] = NF.Exit(exit)
  elseif obs.kind == "ReturnObservation" then
    local projection = Proj.for_exit(state.env)
    local exit = NF.ReturnExit(next_exit_id(state), obs.pc, M.tvalue(obs.value, state.env), projection)
    state.exits[#state.exits + 1] = exit
    state.steps[#state.steps + 1] = NF.Exit(exit)
  elseif obs.kind == "JumpObservation" then
    local exit = NF.JumpExit(next_exit_id(state), obs.pc, obs.offset, Proj.for_exit(state.env))
    state.exits[#state.exits + 1] = exit; state.steps[#state.steps + 1] = NF.Exit(exit)
  elseif obs.kind == "LoopRegionObservation" then
    local exit = NF.LoopRegionExit(next_exit_id(state), obs.region, Proj.for_exit(state.env))
    state.exits[#state.exits + 1] = exit; state.steps[#state.steps + 1] = NF.Exit(exit)
  end
end

function M.normalize(program)
  local state = { env = {}, steps = {}, exits = {}, next_exit_id = 1 }
  for _, eff in ipairs(program.effects or {}) do
    if eff.kind == "DoWrite" and eff.write.kind == "SlotWrite" then
      state.env[eff.write.slot.id] = M.tvalue(eff.write.value, state.env)
    elseif eff.kind == "Observe" then
      lower_observation(eff.observation, state)
    elseif eff.kind == "BarrierAfterStore" then
      state.steps[#state.steps + 1] = NF.Barrier(NF.BarrierAfterStore(M.tvalue(eff.owner, state.env), M.tvalue(eff.value, state.env)))
    end
  end
  if #state.exits == 0 then
    for _, w in ipairs(WriteReduce.final_slot_writes(state.env)) do
      state.steps[#state.steps + 1] = NF.Write(NF.SlotWrite(B.LuaSem.SlotClass(w.slot), w.value))
    end
  end
  return NF.Program(program.slots or {}, state.steps, state.exits)
end

return M

-- lua_nf_key.lua -- stable semantic identity key for LuaNF.

local pvm = require("lalin.pvm")
local M = {}

local function key(v, seen)
  local tv = type(v)
  if tv ~= "table" then return tv .. ":" .. tostring(v) end
  seen = seen or {}; if seen[v] then return "<cycle>" end; seen[v] = true
  local cls = pvm.classof(v)
  if cls and cls.__fields then
    local parts = { tostring(v.kind or (cls.__plan and cls.__plan.name) or cls) }
    for _, f in ipairs(cls.__fields) do parts[#parts + 1] = f.name .. "=" .. key(v[f.name], seen) end
    seen[v] = nil
    return table.concat(parts, "|")
  end
  local parts = {}
  for i = 1, #v do parts[#parts + 1] = key(v[i], seen) end
  seen[v] = nil
  return "[" .. table.concat(parts, ",") .. "]"
end

function M.key(nf) return key(nf) end
return M

-- lua_contract_fact_use.lua -- fact role construction.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local C = B.LuaContract

local M = {}
function M.use(role, subject, predicate, value_key, deps) return C.FactUse(role, subject, predicate, value_key or "", deps or {}) end
function M.checked(subject, predicate, value_key, deps) return M.use(C.Checked, subject, predicate, value_key, deps) end
function M.required(subject, predicate, value_key, deps) return M.use(C.Required, subject, predicate, value_key, deps) end
function M.produced(subject, predicate, value_key, deps) return M.use(C.Produced, subject, predicate, value_key, deps) end
function M.killed(subject, predicate, value_key, deps) return M.use(C.Killed, subject, predicate, value_key, deps) end
return M

-- lua_contract_projection.lua -- exit projection obligations.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local C = B.LuaContract

local M = {}
function M.from_exit(exit) return C.ProjectionObligation(exit, exit.projection or {}) end
return M

-- lua_contract_validate.lua -- contract invariants.

local Validate = require("lua_compile.validate")
local M = {}
function M.validate(contract) return Validate.lua_contract(contract) end
return M

-- lua_contract_key.lua -- contract identity paired with LuaNF key.

local NFKey = require("lua_compile.lua_nf_key")
local M = {}
function M.key(contract) return NFKey.key(contract) end
return M

-- lua_nf_to_lua_contract_derive.lua -- LuaNF.Program -> LuaContract.Contract.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local T = B.T
local NF, C, Fact = T.LuaNF, T.LuaContract, T.LuaFact
local FactUse = require("lua_compile.lua_contract_fact_use")
local Projection = require("lua_compile.lua_contract_projection")
local Dependency = require("lua_compile.lua_contract_dependency")

local M = {}

function M.derive(nf)
  local facts, payloads, projections = {}, {}, {}
  for _, step in ipairs(nf.steps or {}) do
    if step.kind == "Guard" then
      local g = step.guard
      if g.kind == "FactGuard" then
        facts[#facts + 1] = FactUse.required(g.subject, g.predicate, "", g.deps or {})
        facts[#facts + 1] = FactUse.checked(g.subject, g.predicate, "", g.deps or {})
      end
    elseif step.kind == "Write" then
      local w = step.write
      if w.kind == "SlotWrite" then
        local subject = Fact.CanonSlot(w.slot.id)
        facts[#facts + 1] = FactUse.killed(subject, Fact.IsI64, "", {})
        facts[#facts + 1] = FactUse.killed(subject, Fact.IsF64, "", {})
        facts[#facts + 1] = FactUse.killed(subject, Fact.IsTable, "", {})
        if w.value.kind == "BoxI64TValue" then facts[#facts + 1] = FactUse.produced(subject, Fact.IsI64, "", {}) end
        if w.value.kind == "BoxF64TValue" then facts[#facts + 1] = FactUse.produced(subject, Fact.IsF64, "", {}) end
      end
    elseif step.kind == "Exit" then
      projections[#projections + 1] = Projection.from_exit(step.exit)
    end
  end
  for _, exit in ipairs(nf.exits or {}) do projections[#projections + 1] = Projection.from_exit(exit) end
  return C.Contract(C.Transfer(facts, payloads), projections, Dependency.collect_from_facts(facts))
end

return M

-- lua_nf_to_lua_place_plan.lua -- optional LuaNF -> LuaPlace planning boundary.
--
-- Placement is not semantic identity. The default scaffold deliberately returns
-- an empty plan so semantic phases cannot depend on physical locations.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local Place = B.T.LuaPlace
local M = {}
function M.plan(_nf) return Place.Plan({}, {}) end
return M

-- lua_place_projection_plan.lua -- projection realization hints.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local Place = B.T.LuaPlace
local M = {}
function M.already_synced(slot) return Place.AlreadySynced(type(slot) == "table" and slot or B.LuaSem.SlotClass(slot)) end
return M

-- lalin_out_projection.lua -- exit/projection lowering to LalinOut vocabulary.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local Out = B.LalinOut
local M = {}
function M.from_exit(exit) return Out.Projection(exit, exit.projection or {}) end
return M

-- lalin_out_validate.lua -- LalinOut invariants.

local Validate = require("lua_compile.validate")
local M = {}
function M.validate(kernel) return Validate.lalin_out_kernel(kernel) end
return M

-- lua_nf_to_lalin_out_lower.lua -- LuaNF.Program + LuaContract.Contract -> LalinOut.Kernel.

local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local Out = B.LalinOut
local Abi = require("lua_compile.lalin_out_abi")
local Projection = require("lua_compile.lalin_out_projection")

local M = {}

local function has_boundary(nf)
  for _, e in ipairs(nf.exits or {}) do if e.kind == "BoundaryExit" then return true end end
  return false
end

function M.lower(nf, contract)
  local projections = {}
  for _, e in ipairs(nf.exits or {}) do projections[#projections + 1] = Projection.from_exit(e) end
  local kind = has_boundary(nf) and Out.BoundarySpan or Out.InlineSpan
  return Out.Kernel(kind, Abi.default_params(), nf, contract, projections)
end

return M

-- lua_compile_to_normal_form.lua -- LuaCompile.Unit -> NormalForm product.

local Schema = require("lua_compile.schema")
local pvm = require("lalin.pvm")
local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local SemLower = require("lua_compile.lua_src_to_lua_sem_lower")
local NFNormalize = require("lua_compile.lua_sem_to_lua_nf_normalize")
local ContractDerive = require("lua_compile.lua_nf_to_lua_contract_derive")

local T = B.T
local M = {}

function M.compile(unit)
  local sem = SemLower.lower(unit.source, unit.evidence)
  if pvm.classof(sem) == T.LuaSem.Rejected then return T.LuaCompile.Reject(sem.rejection) end
  local nf = NFNormalize.normalize(sem.program)
  local contract = ContractDerive.derive(nf)
  return T.LuaCompile.Ok(T.LuaCompile.NormalForm(nf, contract))
end

return M

-- lua_compile_to_lalin_kernel.lua -- LuaCompile.Unit -> LalinKernel product.

local pvm = require("lalin.pvm")
local B = require("lua_compile.builders")
local ToNF = require("lua_compile.lua_compile_to_normal_form")
local LalinLower = require("lua_compile.lua_nf_to_lalin_out_lower")
local T = B.T

local M = {}

function M.compile(unit)
  local r = ToNF.compile(unit)
  if pvm.classof(r) == T.LuaCompile.Reject then return r end
  local product = r.product
  local kernel = LalinLower.lower(product.nf, product.contract)
  return T.LuaCompile.Ok(T.LuaCompile.LalinKernel(kernel))
end

return M

content.thinking:
**Evaluating guard reduction**

I’m considering whether to reduce guards. It seems like we have guard_reduce available, but it hasn’t been utilized yet. I think tests require that guard success produces verified facts instead of just a reduction. As for the ADDI^4, I realize it only needs one canonical affine value rather than four stores, which means I may have to maintain four guards for one write. Cutting down guards could happen eventually, but I need to ensure the scaffolding is sufficient. I’ll need to check the affine constant as well.

#!/usr/bin/env luajit
package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua;./lua/?.lua;./lua/?/init.lua;" .. package.path

local Schema = require("lua_compile.schema")
local pvm = require("lalin.pvm")
local T = Schema.get({ fresh = true })

assert(T.LuaSrc and T.LuaFact and T.LuaRegion and T.LuaSem and T.LuaNF and T.LuaContract and T.LuaPlace and T.LalinOut and T.LuaCompile)
local op = T.LuaSrc.ADDI(T.LuaSrc.Pc(1), T.LuaSrc.Slot(1), T.LuaSrc.Slot(1), T.LuaSrc.Imm(7))
assert(op.kind == "ADDI")
assert(pvm.classof(op) == T.LuaSrc.ADDI)
local evidence = T.LuaFact.Evidence({}, {}, T.LuaRegion.RegionSet({}))
assert(pvm.classof(evidence) == T.LuaFact.Evidence)
print("ok - SpongeJIT LuaCompile schema")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit
package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua;./lua/?.lua;./lua/?/init.lua;" .. package.path

local Collect = require("lua_compile.lua_src_window_collect")
local Validate = require("lua_compile.lua_src_validate")
local Slots = require("lua_compile.lua_src_slot_alias")

local w = Collect.collect({ { op="LOADI", pc=1, a=1, b=42 }, { op="ADDI", pc=2, a=2, b=1, c=3 }, { op="NOPE", pc=3 } })
assert(#w.ops == 3)
assert(w.ops[1].kind == "LOADI")
assert(w.ops[2].kind == "ADDI")
assert(w.ops[3].kind == "UnsupportedOpcode")
local ok, errs = Validate.validate(w)
assert(ok, table.concat(errs, "\n"))
local inv = Slots.inventory(w)
assert(#inv == 2 and inv[1].id == 1 and inv[2].id == 2)
print("ok - SpongeJIT LuaCompile LuaSrc")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit
package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua;./lua/?.lua;./lua/?/init.lua;" .. package.path

local Runtime = require("lua_compile.lua_fact_from_runtime_observe")
local Foundry = require("lua_compile.lua_fact_from_foundry_bundle")
local Validate = require("lua_compile.lua_fact_validate")
local Contradiction = require("lua_compile.lua_fact_contradiction")
local Payload = require("lua_compile.lua_fact_payload_lease")
local B = require("lua_compile.builders")

local e = Runtime.observe({ { slot=1, predicate="is_i64" }, { const=2, predicate="const_i64", value=7 } })
local ok, errs = Validate.validate(e)
assert(ok, table.concat(errs, "\n"))
assert(#e.observed >= 2)
local f = Foundry.from_bundle({ { subject={kind="slot", id="R3"}, predicate="shape_eq", value="s1" } })
assert(#f.observed >= 2, "closure should imply table fact")
local bad = Runtime.observe({ { slot=1, predicate="is_i64" }, { slot=1, predicate="is_table" } })
assert(#Contradiction.find(bad) == 1)
local lease = Payload.field(B.src_slot_subject(1), B.k(9), B.pc(1), "shape")
assert(lease.kind == "FieldPayload")
print("ok - SpongeJIT LuaCompile LuaFact")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit
package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua;./lua/?.lua;./lua/?/init.lua;" .. package.path

local Collect = require("lua_compile.lua_src_window_collect")
local Recognize = require("lua_compile.lua_src_to_lua_region_recognize")
local Validate = require("lua_compile.lua_region_validate")

local w = Collect.collect({ { op="FORPREP", pc=1, a=4, bx=2 }, { op="LOADI", pc=2, a=8, b=1 }, { op="FORLOOP", pc=4, a=4, bx=3 } })
local rs = Recognize.recognize(w)
assert(#rs.regions == 1)
local r = rs.regions[1]
assert(r.kind == "NumericFor")
assert(r.slots.first.id == 4 and r.slots.last.id == 7)
assert(#r.edges == 4)
local ok, errs = Validate.validate(rs)
assert(ok, table.concat(errs, "\n"))
print("ok - SpongeJIT LuaCompile LuaRegion")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit
package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua;./lua/?.lua;./lua/?/init.lua;" .. package.path

local Collect = require("lua_compile.lua_src_window_collect")
local Observe = require("lua_compile.lua_fact_from_runtime_observe")
local Lower = require("lua_compile.lua_src_to_lua_sem_lower")
local Validate = require("lua_compile.lua_sem_validate")
local T = require("lua_compile.schema").get()

local sem = Lower.lower(Collect.collect({ { op="ADDI", pc=1, a=1, b=1, c=2 } }), Observe.observe({ { slot=1, predicate="is_i64" } }))
assert(sem.kind == "Accepted")
assert(#sem.program.effects == 2, "guard observation + write expected")
local ok, errs = Validate.validate(sem); assert(ok, table.concat(errs, "\n"))
local bad = Lower.lower(Collect.collect({ { op="ADDI", pc=1, a=1, b=1, c=2 } }), Observe.observe({}))
assert(bad.kind == "Rejected" and bad.rejection.reason == T.LuaSem.MissingFact)
local boundary = Lower.lower(Collect.collect({ { op="CALL", pc=3, a=0, b=1, c=1 } }), Observe.observe({}))
assert(boundary.kind == "Accepted")
assert(boundary.program.effects[1].observation.kind == "BoundaryObservation")
print("ok - SpongeJIT LuaCompile LuaSem")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit
package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua;./lua/?.lua;./lua/?/init.lua;" .. package.path

local C = require("lua_compile")
local Key = require("lua_compile.lua_nf_key")
local Validate = require("lua_compile.lua_nf_validate")

local ops = {}
for i=1,4 do ops[#ops+1] = { op="ADDI", pc=i, a=1, b=1, c=1 } end
local r = C.compile_to_normal_form(C.unit_from_events(ops, { { slot=1, predicate="is_i64" } }))
assert(r.kind == "Ok")
local nf = r.product.nf
local write
for _, s in ipairs(nf.steps) do if s.kind == "Write" then assert(not write, "only one final write expected"); write = s.write end end
assert(write and write.value.kind == "BoxI64TValue")
assert(write.value.value.kind == "CanonAffineI64" and write.value.value.constant == 4)
assert(#write.value.value.terms == 1 and write.value.value.terms[1].atom.kind == "SrcSlotI64")
local ok, errs = Validate.validate(nf); assert(ok, table.concat(errs, "\n"))

local function return_value_key(events, observations)
  local rr = C.compile_to_normal_form(C.unit_from_events(events, observations))
  assert(rr.kind == "Ok")
  local exit = rr.product.nf.exits[#rr.product.nf.exits]
  return Key.key(exit.value)
end
local k1 = return_value_key({ {op="ADDI",pc=1,a=1,b=1,c=1}, {op="RETURN1",pc=2,a=1} }, { {slot=1,predicate="is_i64"} })
local k2 = return_value_key({ {op="ADDK",pc=1,a=1,b=1,c=2}, {op="RETURN1",pc=2,a=1} }, { {slot=1,predicate="is_i64"}, {const=2,predicate="const_i64",value=1} })
local k3 = return_value_key({ {op="LOADI",pc=1,a=2,b=1}, {op="ADD",pc=2,a=1,b=1,c=2}, {op="RETURN1",pc=3,a=1} }, { {slot=1,predicate="is_i64"}, {slot=2,predicate="is_i64"} })
assert(k1 == k2 and k2 == k3, "equivalent ADDI/ADDK/ADD return values should share LuaNF shape")
print("ok - SpongeJIT LuaCompile LuaNF")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit
package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua;./lua/?.lua;./lua/?/init.lua;" .. package.path

local C = require("lua_compile")
local Validate = require("lua_compile.lua_contract_validate")
local T = require("lua_compile.schema").get()

local r = C.compile_to_normal_form(C.unit_from_events({ {op="ADDI",pc=1,a=1,b=1,c=1} }, { {slot=1,predicate="is_i64"} }))
assert(r.kind == "Ok")
local contract = r.product.contract
local ok, errs = Validate.validate(contract); assert(ok, table.concat(errs, "\n"))
local checked, killed, produced, projections = false, false, false, #contract.projections
for _, fu in ipairs(contract.transfer.facts) do
  if fu.role == T.LuaContract.Checked and fu.predicate == T.LuaFact.IsI64 then checked = true end
  if fu.role == T.LuaContract.Killed and fu.predicate == T.LuaFact.IsI64 then killed = true end
  if fu.role == T.LuaContract.Produced and fu.predicate == T.LuaFact.IsI64 then produced = true end
end
assert(checked, "guard success must check facts")
assert(killed and produced, "slot write must kill/replace facts")
assert(projections > 0, "guard failure must carry projection obligations")
print("ok - SpongeJIT LuaCompile LuaContract")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit
package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua;./lua/?.lua;./lua/?/init.lua;" .. package.path

local C = require("lua_compile")
local Validate = require("lua_compile.lalin_out_validate")
local Emit = require("lua_compile.lalin_out_emit")
local Schema = require("lua_compile.schema")
local pvm = require("lalin.pvm")
local T = Schema.get()

local r = C.compile_to_lalin_kernel(C.unit_from_events({ {op="ADDI",pc=1,a=1,b=1,c=1} }, { {slot=1,predicate="is_i64"} }))
assert(r.kind == "Ok" and r.product.kind == "LalinKernel")
local kernel = r.product.kernel
local ok, errs = Validate.validate(kernel); assert(ok, table.concat(errs, "\n"))
assert(kernel.kind == T.LalinOut.InlineSpan)
assert(#kernel.params >= 1 and kernel.params[1].name == "ctx")
local function contains_src_op(v, seen)
  if type(v) ~= "table" then return false end
  seen = seen or {}; if seen[v] then return false end; seen[v] = true
  local cls = pvm.classof(v)
  if T.LuaSrc.Op.members[cls] then return true end
  if cls and cls.__fields then for _, f in ipairs(cls.__fields) do if contains_src_op(v[f.name], seen) then return true end end
  elseif not cls then for _, x in pairs(v) do if contains_src_op(x, seen) then return true end end end
  return false
end
assert(not contains_src_op(kernel), "LalinOut must not receive PUC opcode-shaped code")
assert(Emit.emit(kernel):match("LalinOut kernel"))
print("ok - SpongeJIT LuaCompile LalinOut")

#!/usr/bin/env luajit
package.path = "./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?.lua;./experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua;./lua/?.lua;./lua/?/init.lua;" .. package.path

local C = require("lua_compile")
local T = C.schema.get()

local unit = C.unit_from_events({ {op="LOADI",pc=1,a=1,b=9}, {op="RETURN1",pc=2,a=1} }, {})
local nf = C.compile_to_normal_form(unit)
assert(nf.kind == "Ok" and nf.product.kind == "NormalForm")
local mk = C.compile_to_lalin_kernel(unit)
assert(mk.kind == "Ok" and mk.product.kind == "LalinKernel")
assert(mk.product.kernel.normal_form == nf.product.nf, "ASDL identity should share normalized product through interning")
local bad = C.compile_to_normal_form(C.unit_from_events({ {op="GETTABLE",pc=1,a=1,b=2,c=3} }, {}))
assert(bad.kind == "Reject" and bad.rejection.reason == T.LuaSem.UnsupportedOpcode)

local planned = {
"builders.lua", "diagnostics.lua", "errors.lua", "init.lua", "lua_compile_to_lalin_kernel.lua", "lua_compile_to_normal_form.lua", "lua_compile_unit.lua", "lua_compile_validate.lua", "lua_contract_dependency.lua", "lua_contract_fact_use.lua", "lua_contract_key.lua", "lua_contract_projection.lua", "lua_contract_validate.lua", "lua_fact_closure.lua", "lua_fact_contradiction.lua", "lua_fact_from_foundry_bundle.lua", "lua_fact_from_runtime_observe.lua", "lua_fact_payload_lease.lua", "lua_fact_validate.lua", "lua_nf_expr_canonicalize.lua", "lua_nf_guard_reduce.lua", "lua_nf_key.lua", "lua_nf_projection_reduce.lua", "lua_nf_to_lua_contract_derive.lua", "lua_nf_to_lua_place_plan.lua", "lua_nf_to_lalin_out_lower.lua", "lua_nf_validate.lua", "lua_nf_write_reduce.lua", "lua_place_projection_plan.lua", "lua_place_validate.lua", "lua_region_validate.lua", "lua_sem_boundary.lua", "lua_sem_env.lua", "lua_sem_guard.lua", "lua_sem_reject.lua", "lua_sem_to_lua_nf_normalize.lua", "lua_sem_validate.lua", "lua_sem_write.lua", "lua_src_from_puc_decode.lua", "lua_src_slot_alias.lua", "lua_src_to_lua_region_recognize.lua", "lua_src_to_lua_sem_lower.lua", "lua_src_validate.lua", "lua_src_window_collect.lua", "lalin_out_abi.lua", "lalin_out_emit.lua", "lalin_out_projection.lua", "lalin_out_validate.lua", "schema.lua", "validate.lua" }
local seen = {}
for _, f in ipairs(planned) do seen[f] = true end
local p = io.popen("find experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/lua_compile -maxdepth 1 -type f -printf '%f\\n' | sort")
local count = 0
for f in p:lines() do
  count = count + 1
  assert(seen[f], "unplanned LuaCompile file remains: " .. f)
  seen[f] = nil
  assert(not f:match("ssa2"), "ssa2 name forbidden")
end
p:close()
for f in pairs(seen) do error("planned LuaCompile file missing: " .. f) end
assert(count == #planned)
print("ok - SpongeJIT LuaCompile pipeline")

content.thinking:
**Examining test pipeline issues**

I’m looking at a potential issue with the identity of the kernel normalization function across different compile calls. It seems like the ASDL unique Program might be able to intern, especially since it includes arrays. If the parent is unique, it could be canonical, but if there are lists involved, that might complicate things. This sounds a bit flaky, so I'll need to run some tests and make adjustments if it fails.

I want to print the details to see what’s going on.

Task: Continue your LuaCompile implementation work and get the new LuaCompile path back to explicit 85/85 opcode coverage.

Before editing, read the full `experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/ssa_asdl/REWRITE_PLAN.md`, the full ASDL schema `experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/ssa_asdl/spongejit_lua_ssa.asdl`, and the current `experiments/lua_interpreter_vm/spongejit/lua_compile/` files you created.

Goal:
1. `lua_src_from_puc_decode.lua` must explicitly decode all 85 real `LuaSrc.Op` alternatives from the ASDL. Current missing decode entries are: CLOSE, GETI, GETTABLE, GETTABUP, MMBIN, MMBINI, MMBINK, SETI, SETTABLE, SETTABUP, TBC.
2. `lua_src_to_lua_sem_lower.lua` must explicitly handle all 85 real `LuaSrc.Op` alternatives. Handling means one of:
   - consume opcode meaning into LuaSem for simple source ops where semantics are already representable,
   - produce an exact boundary observation only for true VM/language control transfer boundaries,
   - produce a structured `LuaSem.Rejected` for not-yet-supported semantic cases where boundary would be a lie.
3. Add/update a coverage test under the planned test naming scheme, e.g. `test_spongejit_lua_compile_opcode_coverage.lua` if it fits the plan, or extend existing `test_spongejit_lua_compile_lua_src.lua` / `lua_sem.lua`. The test must derive the 85 opcode set from the ASDL/schema or otherwise assert against every ASDL op alternative, not a vague smoke test.
4. Preserve all hard architectural rules: no ssa2, no backward compatibility, no SponDesc, no old bank/materializer/stencil adapter, no opcode helper fallback, unsupported cases are structured rejection.

Important semantic distinction:
- Do not count an unsupported ordinary opcode as a BoundaryObservation just to inflate coverage. Only actual VM/language control-transfer boundaries get BoundaryObservation.
- For not-yet-lowered ordinary opcodes, add explicit structured rejection with a specific reason such as `semantic_not_implemented` / `requires_fact_bundle` as appropriate. The point is explicit 85/85 coverage of the semantic decision table, not fake successful native coverage.

Run all `test_spongejit_lua_compile_*.lua` tests and report:
- decode coverage count,
- semantic decision coverage count,
- files changed,
- test results.
